criteria.name: File exists at exact path with non-empty content
criteria.evidence: docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md exists; 676 lines.

criteria.name: Fast Triage SQL queries topics grouped by publication_status scoped to environment='prod'
criteria.evidence: §2 contains the exact SQL block grouped by publication_status WHERE environment='prod' AND last_published_at >= now() - interval '1 hour'.

criteria.name: §1 provisional banner enumerates TODO(phase-1) items + explicit out-of-scope clause for third mechanism
criteria.evidence: §1 enumerates SIX TODO(phase-1) items (a)-(f) per gate guidance (criterion text said five; gate warnings #1 required the sixth for run-source discriminator). Out-of-scope clause at lines 54-58 explicitly aborts on hybrid/per-guild/per-channel/partial-guild OR missing discriminator.

criteria.name: §3 preconditions includes 'Phase 1 shipped Branch A or Branch B (not a third mechanism)' as a gate
criteria.evidence: §3 table contains 'Phase 1 shipped Branch A or Branch B' AND 'Run-source discriminator present' as separate gate rows, plus 'All SIX Phase-1 TODOs resolved' row.

criteria.name: GO/TUNE/ABORT shadow-mode rubric keyed on listed signals with Phase-1 shadow partials from telemetry
criteria.evidence: §4 table covers replay-divergence, collision-override rate, record_observation, Phase-1 shadow-attempted-publish partials (annotated as sourced from telemetry NOT topics WHERE environment='dev'), schema-rejection rate, cost/run, latency/run.

criteria.name: Publishing-flag-flip procedure with placeholder, branch A/B verification, next-observed-run anchoring
criteria.evidence: §5 contains 8-step procedure using <LIVE_EDITOR_PUBLISH_TOGGLE>; step 4 polls live_update_editor_runs until status='completed' (not a fixed window) noting hourly cadence per :633.

criteria.name: Branch B verification names Discord-side count as PRIMARY signal, SQL marker as nice-to-have secondary
criteria.evidence: §5 Branch B explicitly labels 'Primary signal (Discord-side count)' and demotes SQL suppression marker to 'Secondary, nice-to-have' with explicit fallback note.

criteria.name: Verification does NOT check 'zero new rows in legacy live_update_feed_items'
criteria.evidence: §5 contains explicit 'Do not use "zero new rows in legacy live_update_feed_items" as a verification signal. Per :651...'

criteria.name: Four partial-publish detection SQL queries + prod-only trip-wire count + Phase-1 telemetry instruction
criteria.evidence: §6 contains queries (a)-(d) scoped prod with dev-swap comments; prod-only trip-wire SQL `prod_partials_since_cutover`; explicit instruction to add Phase-1 shadow telemetry count NOT from topics WHERE environment='dev', citing :611/:627/:633.

criteria.name: Decision tree maps partial-publish trip-wire to cumulative ≥ 2 → ABORT + restore topic_publications citing :600-601
criteria.evidence: §7 second row: '(Phase-1 telemetry shadow partials) + (Phase-2 prod partials from §6) cumulative ≥ 2 per :600-601 → ABORT + restore topic_publications'.

criteria.name: Decision tree splits lease symptoms into three distinct rows AND includes third-mechanism row
criteria.evidence: §7 has three lease-symptom rows (self-contention HOT-FIX, legacy writing to NEW tables ABORT, run-row overlap ABORT) and a final third-mechanism / discriminator-absent ABORT row.

criteria.name: Rollback leaves topics untouched, distinguishes pre/post-rename, cites :596, covers Branch-A/B verification
criteria.evidence: §8 has pre-rename variant (5 steps, step 5 'Leave topics rows alone' citing :596) and post-rename variant (toggle + reverse-rename via §9 chain); both Branch-A and Branch-B verification shapes documented.

criteria.name: Table-rename migration renames six legacy tables to _legacy_<name>, retains live_update_editor_runs, BEGIN/COMMIT with dry-run note, row-count parity, constraint preservation
criteria.evidence: §9 migration body contains the six ALTER TABLE...RENAME TO statements inside begin/commit with 'swap to rollback; for dry-run preview' comment; explicit 'live_update_editor_runs is NOT renamed' comment; pre/post UNION ALL row-count queries; constraint-preservation note for indexes/sequences/FK/RLS.

criteria.name: Names full migration hand-off chain
criteria.evidence: §9 hand-off chain (4 numbered steps): author in .migrations_staging/ → copy verbatim to /Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/ → supabase db push → verify in supabase_migrations.schema_migrations.

criteria.name: §9 cross-checks against redesign.md:212-223 + storage_handler.py:652-1968 NOT legacy-boundaries.md
criteria.evidence: §9 migration body comment explicitly: 'per docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:212-223 (cross-checked against src/common/storage_handler.py:652-1968 — not docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md, which is about daily_summaries)'.

criteria.name: Rename runs only AFTER 24-48h watch passes
criteria.evidence: §9 opens 'Run this only after the 24-48h watch has passed cleanly'; §8 reiterates 'Run §9 rename only after the 24-48h watch passes. This keeps rollback to a single toggle flip during the watch window.'

criteria.name: Deletion section with operator-filled blank, DROP TABLE via same hand-off, bring-forward/push-out circumstances
criteria.evidence: §10 'Rename applied on: __________' and '_legacy_* deletion scheduled for: __________' with 'Do not hardcode a date in this doc' instruction; DROP migration via same hand-off chain; bring-forward and push-out circumstances enumerated.

criteria.name: References redesign.md for schema + ABORT footer
criteria.evidence: §11 lists 14 named redesign anchors and 3 trip-wire signals; redesign.md referenced throughout for schema shape, not duplicated.

criteria.name: References _publish_accepted_candidates / _select_publishable_candidates as legacy fallback path
criteria.evidence: §1 Primary tools, §5 Branch B, §7 duplicate-post HOT-FIX row, and §8 step 3 all reference live_update_editor.py:84-107 and :261.

criteria.name: Document length comparable to runbook-payments.md (~400 lines, 200-700 acceptable)
criteria.evidence: 676 lines — at upper bound of acceptable range; budget driven by 6 SQL query blocks, 12-row precondition table, 15-row decision tree, and migration body.

criteria.name: Tone matches runbook-payments.md — terse, imperative, copy-paste SQL ready
criteria.evidence: Subjective judgment: Fast Triage opener, table-driven preconditions/rubric/decision-tree, copy-paste SQL blocks, minimal narrative prose — matches the runbook-payments pattern.

criteria.name: Every TODO(phase-1) placeholder clearly labeled (criterion says five; gate guidance overrode to six)
criteria.evidence: Six TODO(phase-1) markers (a)-(f) enumerated in §1; gate warning #1 explicitly required adding the sixth for run-source discriminator. Spirit of criterion (clear labeling) is met.

criteria.name: No code edits outside the runbook; tests unchanged
criteria.evidence: Only docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md was added; executor commands show no edits to source code or tests.

summary: Approved. The 676-line runbook implements all 8 settled decisions (SD-1 through SD-8) and the gate warnings (especially the sixth TODO(phase-1) f for the run-source discriminator). All 11 critique flags including the previously-open FLAG-011 are resolved by the document content: §1 enumerates six TODO(phase-1) items, §3 has the discriminator-present gate, §5 names Branch-B Discord-side count as primary signal and anchors verification on next observed run, §6 trip-wire is two-sourced and prod-only on the SQL side, §7 splits lease symptoms into three rows plus third-mechanism ABORT row, §8 leaves topics untouched citing :596, §9 names the full migration hand-off chain with correct cross-reference to redesign.md:212-223 + storage_handler.py:652-1968. Length is at the upper bound (676 lines) of the acceptable 200-700 range; tone matches runbook-payments.md.

task_verdicts.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Audit confirmed output path was unused, anchors re-read, and the absent run-source discriminator column on live_update_editor_runs flagged as the sixth TODO(phase-1).

Pass. §1 banner enumerates SIX TODO(phase-1) items with explicit third-mechanism abort clause; §2 Fast Triage SQL scopes environment='prod' with the dev-swap comment and the no-partials-in-dev annotation.

Pass. §3 precondition table includes both 'Phase 1 shipped Branch A or Branch B' and 'Run-source discriminator present' as separate gates; §4 rubric annotates partial-publish row as sourced from Phase-1 telemetry NOT topics WHERE environment='dev'.

Pass. §5 Branch B labels Discord-side count PRIMARY and SQL marker secondary; anchors verification on next observed run; explicit ban on 'zero new rows in legacy live_update_feed_items' check. §6 uses prod-only SQL count + explicit Phase-1 telemetry instruction.

Pass. §7 splits lease symptoms into three distinct rows and includes a third-mechanism / discriminator-absent ABORT row; §8 leaves topics rows alone citing :596 and distinguishes pre-rename from post-rename rollback variants with Branch-A and Branch-B verification.

Pass. §9 names the full migration hand-off chain (staging → workspace migrations → supabase db push → verify in supabase_migrations.schema_migrations), renames the correct six legacy tables, retains live_update_editor_runs, uses BEGIN/COMMIT with dry-run note, has pre/post row-count UNION ALL, and includes constraint-preservation note. §10 uses the same chain for DROP and leaves the deletion date as an operator-filled blank.
Pass. §11 footer lists 14 named redesign anchors and re-states the three trip-wire signals from :599-604. Self-review polish pass items all met.

sense_check_verdicts.verdict: Confirmed. Output path was unused; anchors re-read; the absence of any run_source/editor_source/run_type column on live_update_editor_runs justifies the sixth TODO(phase-1).
Confirmed. §1 banner lists six TODO(phase-1) items with explicit out-of-scope clause; §2 Fast Triage SQL is prod-scoped with the correct annotations.
Confirmed. §3 includes both Branch A/B gate and run-source-discriminator-present gate; §4 partial-publish row annotated to source from Phase-1 telemetry not topics WHERE environment='dev'.

Confirmed. §5 Branch B names Discord-side count as PRIMARY and SQL marker as secondary; anchors on next observed run; explicit ban on legacy live_update_feed_items check. §6 trip-wire is prod-only SQL + Phase-1 telemetry instruction.

Confirmed. §7 splits lease symptoms into three rows; partial-publish trip-wire phrased exactly as required citing :600-601; third-mechanism row covers run-source-discriminator-absent. §8 leaves topics rows alone citing :596; pre- and post-rename rollback are distinct with both Branch A and Branch B verification.

Confirmed. §9 names the full migration hand-off chain in four numbered steps; renames the six legacy tables to _legacy_<name>; retains live_update_editor_runs with inline comment; BEGIN/COMMIT with dry-run rollback note; pre/post row-count UNION ALL; constraint-preservation note; six-table cross-reference cites :212-223 and storage_handler.py:652-1968, explicitly NOT legacy-boundaries.md. §10 DROP uses the same hand-off chain and the deletion date is an operator-filled blank.

Confirmed. §11 footer lists the 14 named redesign anchors and re-lists the three trip-wire signals from :599-604. Self-review polish pass items met: 676-line length is at upper bound of acceptable 200-700 range, six TODO(phase-1) markers visibly labeled, _legacy_* deletion date is an operator-filled blank, tone matches runbook-payments.md.
